title: teucrium pumilum subsp. verticillatum
intro: Teucrium pumilum subsp. verticillatum, commonly known as the Alpine Germander or Alpine Teucrium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, including indigestion, bloating, and diarrhea, due to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Teucrium pumilum subsp. verticillatum include its ability to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent to create a concentrated extract.
